steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the poem. The poem dedicates itself to Puerto Cabello and is built around a single structural contrast: the "while outside... raving" storm versus Puerto Cabello's calm, land-locked waters where a canoe "gently glides."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It contrasts the tranquility of the waters near Puerto Cabello with the roughness of waters elsewhere.': Matches the poem's core structure exactly — outside waters are stormy, Puerto Cabello's are warm and easy. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It emphasizes the severity of a storm that is expected to arrive soon in Puerto Cabello.': The storm is raging outside Puerto Cabello, not approaching it. The poem gives no indication the storm will reach the town. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It compares the sturdiness of two types of boats.': The poem mentions a "ship" driven on a desert shore and a "pirogue" (canoe) gliding gently, but makes no claim about the sturdiness of either — boat construction is not the subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It describes the speaker's appreciation for the people of Puerto Cabello.': No people of Puerto Cabello are mentioned or described anywhere in the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
